message:
perf: fix message ordering in agent loop and pre-allocate session vectors

Agent message loop (run_agent_loop):
- Push messages to self.messages BEFORE emitting MessageStart/MessageEnd
  events, ensuring event handlers see consistent message state. Previously
  the push happened between Start and End, which could cause handlers
  observing MessageStart to see stale message history.
- Restructure ToolExecutionEnd emission to clone content/details from
  the already-constructed Arc<ToolResultMessage> instead of from the raw
  tool output, avoiding a redundant deep clone of potentially large tool
  output content.
- Simplify the prompt/pending_messages iteration by removing intermediate
  `end_msg` bindings — the final `push(msg)` now moves the value after
  the last clone, which is equivalent but clearer.

Session message reconstruction (get_model_messages):
- Replace `Vec::new()` with `Vec::with_capacity(path_len)` in both
  the compaction and non-compaction code paths, eliminating incremental
  reallocations when rebuilding the message list from session entries.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
